english: mdaṅs-kyi (mdangs kyi) kluṅ (klung) tsáṅ-ma (tsang ma) all the fields belonging to Kardang (n. of a village).
pf. klubs (klubs), to cover, e.g. the body with ornaments Pth.

I. sbst. earwax Sch. - II. vb., pf. (b)klags ((b)klags), ft. (b)klag ((b)klag), imp. klog (klog), lhogs (lhogs), to read, B., C., yid-kyis (yid kyis) klóg-pa (klog pa) to read without uttering a sound; klog-pa (klog pa) or klog (klog) sbst. reading, klog bzáṅ-po (klog bzang po) śés-pa (shes pa) Mil. to be a good reader; klóg-gi slób-dpon (klog gi slob dpon) a reading-teacher. - klóg-gra (klog gra) a reading-school. - klóg-tabs (klog thabs), klóg-tsul (klog tshul) art, way of reading.

acc. to Lex. = Ssk. urmi, wave; in the living language it is used for middle; in ancient literature for expanse, esp. nám-mḱai (nam mkha'i) of the heavens, rgyá-mtsoi (rgya mtsho'i) of the sea; raṅ-byúṅ (rang byung) kloṅ yaṅs (klong yangs) brjód-méd (brjod med) the unspeakably vast uncreated space; hence: the space of heaven, the heavens, klóṅ-du (klong du) ldiṅ-ba (lding ba) to soar, to hover in the sky. This vagueness of meaning makes the word suited to the idle fancies of mysticism, as in: klóṅ-du gyúr-ba (klong du gyur ba), which seems to denote a soaring into mystic perfection. - dba-klóṅ (dba klong) Sch.: wave; Tibetans of to-day, and Schr.: the midst of the waves. - kloṅ-brdól (klong brdol) Glr. was explained by Lamas: emerging from amidst the waves. (The significations 'depth, abyss, plenty, body' added by Sch. seem to be erroneous). Cf. dkyel (dkyel).
also kluṅ-pa (klung pa), to mend, patch v. also lhán-pa (lhan pa)
Ssk. prop. phthisis pulmonalis; but acc. to Tib. pathology kśá-ya nág-po (k+Sha ya nag po) denotes a bilious disease, prob. icterus niger, black jaundice.

also rkan (rkan) (Ld. *skan* (*skan*)) 1. the palate, yá-dkan (ya dkan), the upper, má-dkan (ma dkan) the lower part of the palate; *káṅ-ḍa déb-pa* (*kang ḍa deb pa*) to smack C.; dkan-yñér (dkan gnyer) the wrinkles of the roof of the mouth Cs. -- 2. dkan yzár-po (dkan gzar po) Lex. w. c, Sch. steep declivity, precipice.

1. adj., also -bo (-bo), seld. -mo (-mo), difficult, slób-pa dká-ba (slob pa dka' ba) learning is difficult Dzl., gen. with supine: slób-tu (slob tu) or slób-par dka (slob par dka') it is difficult to learn, or with the root: go-dká (go dka') difficult to understand; dká-ba byuṅ (dka' ba byung) it has become difficult, it is difficult (to me, to him etc.) -- 2. sbst. dká-ba (dka' ba) pains, exertion, hardship, suffering, dká-ba méd-par (dka' ba med par) without difficulty, easily, dka-ba spyod-pa (dka' ba spyod pa) to undergo hardships = to use exercises of penance. dka-grél (dka' 'grel) Cs. 'a difficult commentary', acc. to Tib. dictionaries = पञ्जिका (pañjikā) perpetual commentary, lit.: explanation of difficulties. -- dka-túb (dka' thub), dka-spyód (dka' spyod), dka-spyád (dka' spyad) 1. penance. 2. penitent; dka-túb-pa (dka' thub pa), dka-spyód-pa (dka' spyod pa), dká-túb-ċan (dka' thub can), penitent, tsó-ba dka-túb-la rtén-pa (tsho ba dka' thub la rten pa) to live as a penitent. -- dka-sdúg (dka' sdug) trouble, dka-sdúg máṅ-po byéd-pa (dka' sdug mang po byed pa) to take great pains C. -- dka-tségs (dka' tshegs) = dká-ba (dka' ba). -- dka-lás (dka' las) 1. a troublesome work. 2. trouble, distress.

I. adj., also -po (-po), seld. -mo (-mo) 1. white, whitish, gray. - 2. morally good, standing on the side of virtue -- 3. candid, sincere? las dkár-po (las dkar po) good action; ḱa-zàs dkár-po (kha zas dkar po) v. dkar-zás (dkar zas); dkár-la dmar-mdáṅs-ċan (dkar la dmar mdangs can) white and red of complexion Pth. II. sbst. whiteness. -- dkár-mo (dkar mo) sbst. 1. the goddess Durga. -- 2. white rice Cs. Comp. dkar-skya (dkar skya) light-gray. -- dkar-ḱúṅ (dkar khung) 1. window-hole in a wall W. -- 2. opening for the smoke in the roof C. -- dkar-goṅ (dkar gong) C. a piece of quartz, (acc. to popular belief porcelain is made of quartz,) hence Cs.: 'porcelain-clay.' -- dkar-rgyá (dkar rgya) rose-coloured. -- dkar-ċág (dkar chag) register. -- dkar-tóg (dkar tog) = dkar-zás (dkar zas). -- dkar-mé (dkar me) a light (?), dkar-mé sbor-ba (dkar me sbor ba) Sch. 'to light a candle.' -- dkar-dmar (dkar dmar) light-red Sch. -- dkar-rtsi (dkar rtsi) white-wash, consisting of lime or some other earthy colour C., W. -- dkar-zás (dkar zas), ḱa-zás dkar-po (kha zas dkar po), dkar-tóg (dkar tog) clean food, lenter
